governing_design.constraint: data-only comparison; no generic Phase 0.85 marker authority authored

continuity_prior_a2_n1.use_in_this_comparison: historical gap continuity; not a direct launchd check-in data point

shared_facts.reason: Both accepted records contain launchd-produced evidence that check-in returned a materialized response.
shared_facts.strictness_note: N2 records result=dict; A2 records reply presence but not the response type, so result=dict is not promoted from n=2 by this comparison.

shared_facts.reason: Both accepted records contain launchd-produced evidence that the check-in reply exposed a MachServices dictionary.
shared_facts.strictness_note: N2 records type=dict; A2 records dictionary presence only, so type=dict remains a richer N2 field until extraction can bind both consumers to it.

shared_facts.reason: Both accepted records contain launchd-produced evidence that the selected service had a MachServices entry.
shared_facts.strictness_note: The selected service name is an extraction parameter. N2 records service/type in the launchd marker; A2 binds service name through the accepted A2 service context rather than the service-entry marker itself.

shared_facts.reason: Both accepted records contain launchd-produced evidence that the selected MachServices entry yielded a usable receive right.
shared_facts.strictness_note: N2 records right=receive in the marker; A2 records positive receive port plus usability. Generic extraction should preserve the common receive-right materialization fact and separately decide whether all consumers must emit a right kind field.

consumer_specific_facts.reason: A2 launch check-in call/key markers are harness-produced and cannot satisfy a generic launchd-produced fact.
N2 has a launchd-produced check-in terminal marker. A2 has a harness subclaim summary instead, so this is not shared at n=2.
A2 subclaim summary is harness orchestration, not a launchd-produced terminal fact.
A2 proves port identity with an ASL nonce-bearing OOL _asl_server_message payload.
N2 proves notifyd behavior through notify-native register/post/check/TWQ observations and selected kernel receive facts.
Cross-process client-death observation is outside the accepted narrowed N2C-1/N2C-2a/N2C-3 claim and is not a launchd-handoff fact.
deferred_markers: SOURCE_CREATE registered_name to DEAD_EVENT registered_name correlation

service_name.generic_requirement: service name is a parameter, not a shared literal

fixture_form.generic_requirement: Phase 0.85 extraction must identify boolean vs dictionary MachServices forms explicitly before claiming both

identity_instrument.notifyd_n2: notify-native register/post/check observation plus kernel receive facts
identity_instrument.generic_requirement: port identity proof is consumer-selected; generic handoff may require an abstract identity-proof slot

residual_gaps: No generic Phase 0.85 marker authority exists yet.
N2C-2b cross-process client-death observation remains deferred and is not a launchd-handoff fact.
N2 records richer response/type/right fields than A2 for some launchd facts; extraction must decide whether to add or require equivalent A2 fields before promoting those richer policies.
The n=2 set does not prove launchd cleanup, remove/reload, stale service-port invalidation, ResetAtClose behavior, or broad launchd resource parity.
The n=2 set does not prove PID 1 launchd behavior or D22/D23 launchctl lifecycle authority.
Harness-produced fixture/service-name/orchestration markers remain supporting provenance only and cannot become generic :launchd producer facts.
